prompt:
Passage 1
A regional building-services company recently updated the way it handles requests for replacement light bulbs. Previously, employees sent emails to the maintenance department when lights burned out. These emails did not always include the exact location, so maintenance staff sometimes had to walk through several rooms before finding the problem.
The company introduced a short online lighting request form. Employees now enter the building, floor, room number, fixture location, and whether the area is a hallway, office, storage room, or work area. The form also asks whether the burned-out light affects safety or normal work.
Maintenance staff say the new form helps them plan their route and bring the correct supplies. Managers report that lighting issues are being resolved with fewer return trips.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Employees stopped reporting burned-out lights.
choices.B: A new lighting request form helped maintenance staff respond more efficiently.
choices.C: Maintenance staff now replace lights only in hallways.
choices.D: The company removed all lighting from storage rooms.

explanation: The passage explains how the online lighting request form gives maintenance staff better location details and helps them resolve issues with fewer return trips.

prompt:
Passage 2
NOTICE
Beginning May 20, all employees using the shared material scale must clean the weighing surface after each use. Employees should also report any display errors or unstable readings to the quality lead.
The updated procedure was created after several batches had to be weighed again because leftover material remained on the scale surface. Even small amounts of residue can affect the next measurement.
Cleaning wipes will be stored beside the scale, and the quality lead will review proper scale-care steps during shift meetings.

What is the purpose of the notice?

choices.A: To explain a new procedure for using and cleaning the shared material scale
choices.B: To cancel all weighing procedures
choices.D: To move the scale to the parking lot

explanation: The notice explains how employees should clean and report issues with the shared material scale.

prompt:
Passage 3
Many companies use delivery checklists to reduce mistakes when sending products to customers. A checklist may remind employees to confirm the customer name, delivery address, order number, package count, and any special instructions.
Checklists are especially useful when orders look similar. Two customers may order the same product but require different quantities or delivery locations. A final checklist gives employees a chance to catch differences before a truck leaves the facility.
However, checklists are only useful when employees treat them as more than a formality. If workers mark each line without actually checking the order, the process may not prevent errors. Supervisors often remind employees that a checklist is a tool for careful review, not just paperwork.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Delivery checklists help reduce errors when employees use them carefully.
choices.B: Delivery addresses should not be checked.
choices.C: Similar orders are always impossible to tell apart.
choices.D: Checklists are useful only after the truck leaves.

explanation: The passage explains that delivery checklists reduce errors when employees use them carefully as a final review.

prompt:
Passage 4
Before modern water meters became common, many communities charged residents for water based on flat fees or rough estimates. This system did not always reflect how much water a household actually used.
Water meters changed utility billing by measuring actual water use. Customers who used less water could be billed differently from customers who used more. Meter readings also helped utilities detect unusual usage that might suggest a leak.
Modern meters may be read manually or through automated systems. Although meter technology has improved, utilities still need accurate records, regular maintenance, and clear communication when unusual usage is found.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Water meters helped utilities measure use more accurately and identify possible problems.
choices.B: Flat fees always show exact water use.
choices.C: Water meters are used only in factories.
choices.D: Utilities no longer need customer records.

explanation: The passage focuses on how water meters improved measurement of actual use and helped utilities identify unusual usage.

prompt:
Passage 5
A regional office recently introduced a new process for handling incoming mail. Previously, mail was placed in several department bins throughout the building. If an envelope was delivered to the wrong bin, it could remain unnoticed for several days.
The new process uses one central mail station. Mail is sorted each morning by a designated employee, and departments receive a notification when important documents arrive. Items that require a signature or time-sensitive response are logged before being delivered.
Managers say the new system has reduced misplaced documents and made it easier to track important mail. Some departments initially preferred their old bins, but most agreed that the central station improved accountability.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The office stopped receiving mail.
choices.B: A central mail station improved mail tracking and reduced misplaced documents.
choices.C: Departments now sort all mail at random.
choices.D: Important documents are no longer logged.

explanation: The passage explains that the central mail station reduced misplaced documents and improved tracking of important mail.

prompt:
Passage 6
Many workplaces use refresher training to review skills or procedures employees have already learned. Refresher training may be scheduled yearly, after a policy change, or when supervisors notice repeated mistakes.
The purpose is not to treat employees as beginners. Instead, refresher training helps workers remember important steps, learn updates, and correct habits that may have drifted over time. Even experienced employees can benefit from reviewing procedures they do often.
Good refresher training is focused. If workers need a review of one updated procedure, a short targeted session may be more useful than repeating an entire long course.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Refresher training helps employees review important procedures and updates.
choices.B: Refresher training is useful only for brand-new employees.
choices.D: Long courses are always better than focused sessions.

explanation: The passage explains that refresher training helps employees review important steps, learn updates, and correct drifting habits.

prompt:
Passage 7
Many communities install public benches near sidewalks, transit stops, parks, and civic buildings. Benches give people a place to rest, wait, or gather. They can be especially helpful for older adults, people with limited mobility, and parents with young children.
The location of benches matters. A bench placed near shade, lighting, and clear walking paths is usually more useful than one placed where it blocks movement or feels unsafe. Communities may also consider nearby trash cans, bus routes, and building entrances when choosing locations.
Benches require maintenance. Broken boards, sharp edges, graffiti, or loose hardware can reduce safety and comfort. Regular inspections help keep public seating useful and welcoming.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.A: Public benches can improve access and comfort when well placed and maintained.
choices.B: Communities should remove benches from parks.
choices.C: Benches are useful only inside private homes.
choices.D: Public seating never requires inspection.

explanation: The passage explains that benches help public access and comfort, but location and maintenance determine how useful they are.

prompt:
Passage 8
An apprenticeship preparation program recently added short “direction check” exercises to its weekly practice. Instructors noticed that some students lost points because they answered what they expected the question to ask rather than what it actually asked.
During the exercises, students underline command words such as “compare,” “identify,” “infer,” or “explain.” They then restate the task in their own words before choosing an answer. The activity takes only a few minutes but is repeated often.
Instructors believe this habit helps students slow down at the right moment. The goal is not to make students read every passage more slowly, but to make sure they understand the task before answering.

Why did the program add direction check exercises?

choices.A: Some students answered what they expected instead of what the question actually asked.
choices.B: Students no longer needed weekly practice.
choices.C: Instructors wanted to remove command words.
choices.D: The program stopped using reading questions.

explanation: The exercises were added because some students answered based on expectation instead of the actual question.

explanation: The passage supports the idea that understanding the exact task helps students answer more accurately.
